title.en: Hit-and-Run Accident? The Government Compensation Scheme — ₹2 Lakh for Death, ₹50,000 for Injury

answerBox.en: When the offending vehicle is never traced, victims of hit-and-run accidents claim from the central government scheme under Section 161 MV Act: ₹2,00,000 for death and ₹50,000 for grievous injury (rates from April 2022). File the FIR immediately, then apply to the district Claims Enquiry Officer with the FIR and medical/post-mortem records. If the vehicle is traced later, a full MACT claim opens.

en.label: FIR at the local station
en.startsFrom: record it as hit-and-run, vehicle untraced

en.label: If vehicle traced later
en.startsFrom: full MACT claim; scheme amount adjusts

en.title: FIR first — insist on "vehicle untraced"
en.summary: The scheme exists precisely for unidentified vehicles.
en.detail: Report immediately and ensure the FIR records the accident and that the **vehicle fled unidentified**. The police's final report confirming the vehicle remained untraced is the key document for the scheme. Collect witness details and any CCTV leads — if the vehicle IS traced, you exit the capped scheme and file a full, uncapped [MACT claim](/help/vehicle-accident/accident-compensation-mact) instead, which is better.

en.title: Get the injury/death documented precisely
en.detail: For injuries, the hospital record and medico-legal certificate should establish **grievous hurt** (fractures, permanent damage, 20+ days hospitalisation — the IPC/BNS grievous-hurt definitions apply). For death, the post-mortem report and death certificate. The driver who fled faces prosecution — **Section 304A IPC (now Section 106 BNS**, with the stricter hit-and-run clause in 106(2)) — but your compensation does not wait for him to be found.

en.title: Apply under the scheme to the district officer
en.summary: Claims Enquiry Officer → District Committee → insurer-run fund pays.
en.detail: File the scheme application (Compensation to Victims of Hit and Run Motor Accidents Scheme, 2022) with the **Claims Enquiry Officer** of your district (SDM/taluk office handles it in most states) attaching FIR, police untraced report, medical/PM records, and legal-heir proof for death claims. The District Level Committee sanctions and the designated insurer (GIC-administered fund) pays **₹2,00,000 (death) / ₹50,000 (grievous injury)** into your bank account. Our ₹199 documentation service prepares the application set.

en.title: Track it — and keep the MACT door open
en.summary: Follow up with the committee; traced vehicles reopen full claims.
en.detail: The scheme contemplates disposal within a few months — follow up in writing with the enquiry officer and committee. If police later trace the vehicle, file the **MACT claim within 6 months of tracing**; the scheme amount already received is deducted from the tribunal award, never forfeited. Beware touts offering to "arrange" scheme money for a cut — the process is free and direct.

rows.label: Scheme application
rows.note: No fee at any stage; payment to your bank account

en.footnote: Simple injuries (non-grievous) are outside the scheme. Amounts and procedure are set by the 2022 central scheme — verify current figures at the enquiry office; some states add ex-gratia on top.

en: hit and run case me muavza
hit and run 2 lakh compensation apply

en.q: Who pays hit-and-run compensation if there is no insurer?
en.a: A central fund administered through the General Insurance Council/designated insurers under Section 161 MV Act — built from insurance contributions precisely for untraced-vehicle victims. The sanctioning happens through your district's Claims Enquiry Officer and District Level Committee.

en.q: What counts as "grievous injury" for the ₹50,000?
en.a: The grievous-hurt categories of criminal law: fractures, permanent privation of sight/hearing, loss of limb or joint use, disfiguration, or injuries causing 20+ days of severe bodily pain/hospitalisation. The medico-legal certificate's wording controls — ensure the treating doctor records the classification.

en.q: Is there a deadline for the scheme application?
en.a: The 2022 scheme expects claims within a reasonable period and committees can entertain explained delays, but practically: apply within 6 months of the police confirming the vehicle untraced. Sooner filings sail; stale ones invite avoidable scrutiny.

en.q: The police traced the vehicle after I took the scheme money. What now?
en.a: Good news — file the full MACT claim within 6 months of tracing. The ₹2 lakh/₹50,000 already paid is simply deducted from the tribunal award; a genuine death claim commonly computes to many times the scheme amount, so tracing is worth pushing the police on.

en.q: The fleeing driver — what does the new law do to him?
en.a: Section 106(1) BNS punishes death by rash/negligent driving (up to 5 years); Section 106(2) targets fleeing without reporting — up to 10 years. The stricter clause's enforcement has been politically deferred amid transporter protests, but prosecution under 106(1)/304A-era law proceeds regardless. Your compensation is independent of his trial.

reviewer: Adv. Arjun Nair, Bar Council of Kerala, KER/0973/2012
author: WakilBhai Editorial Team
